properties.remark: MULTIPLE STRUCTURES WITH ROOFS GONE AND TRAILERS FLIPPED OVER. ONE PERSON RESCUED FROM INSIDE THE TRAILER. A DEFINITE 100 YARD PATH OF DAMAGE OCCURRED.
STREETLIGHT BLOWN DOWN.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL AND 60 MPH WINDS.
WIND GUSTS OVER 55 MPH.
BROKEN WINDOWS IN SOME HOMES.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM ZEPHYR. THE REPORT WAS OBTAINED THROUGH TWITTER.
THE REPORT WAS OBTAINED THROUGH TWITTER.
ROUGHLY 1 MIN OF PING-PONG SIZE HAIL AND ABOUT ANOTHER MINUTE OR 2 OF NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED BY THE ALON STATION ON 67 IN MERTZON, TX.
QUARTER TO HALF-DOLLAR SIZE HAIL.
REPORTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA.
VIDEO POSTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA.
LOTS OF QUARTER SIZE WITH SOME GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM BROOKESMITH. PUBLIC SENT A PICTURE OF BASEBALL SIZED HAIL OVER SOCIAL MEDIA ALONG WITH A REPORT OF BROKEN WINDSHIELD AND SIDING DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
THE WEST TEXAS MESONET STATION MEASURED A GUST TO 61 MPH.
THE PUBLIC REPORTED 1 TO 1.5 INCH HAIL.
SAN ANGELO ASOS REPORTED 64 KT (74 MPH) WIND GUST.
REPORTED BYTHROCKMORTON CTY SHERIFF OFFICE.
REPORT FROM MPING: GOLF BALL (1.75 IN.).
TREES BLOWN DOWN, POWER LINES DOWN, ROOF BLOWN OFF BARN.
NEAR BEND, TX.
BASEBALL SIZE HAIL.
GOLFBALL HAIL.
CAR STALLED IN FLOODED INTERSECTION OF OAK GROVE BLVD AND GREEN MEADOW.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED AT LAKE NASWORTHY.

METAL BARN ROOF PEELED OFF, LARGE PECAN TREE UPROOTED.

TREES ALSO BLOWN DOWN, AND FLOODING INTO A GARAGE.
FLOODED GARAGE, BATHROOM, BEDROOM. ALSO STREET. TORRENTIAL RAIN MIXED WITH HAIL.

70 MPH WINDS. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
MULTIPLE TREES AND BRANCHES DOWN IN THE ROAD.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL.

GOLFBALL HAIL.
REPORT FROM MPING: QUARTER (1.00 IN.).
REPORTED BY A WEST TEXAS MESONET STATION LOCATED 7 MILES NW OF SAN ANGELO.
WEST TEXAS MESONET MEASURED AN 81 MPH WIND GUST.
SAN SABA AWOS REPORTED A 67 MPH WIND GUST.
SAN SABA AWOS REPORTED A 67 MPH WIND GUST. SO ALSO REPORTED POWER LINES DOWN IN SAN SABA.
ROOF DAMAGE REPORTED AT LIQUOR STORE AT HIGHWAY 67 AND FM 381 IN ROWENA. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED.

THE PUBLIC REPORTED DAMAGE FORM A MICROBURST. THE DAMAGE INCLUDED ROOF DAMAGE TO AN ADOBE BUILDING, A CARPORT WAS BLOWN OVER, A FEW MESQUITE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND LARGE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED FROM AN 160 YEAR OLD LIVE OAK TREE. ALSO, A POWER POLE WAS LEANING. THE ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 95 MPH. THE LOCATION IS 11 MILES WEST OF SAN ANGELO NEAR BRUSHY CREEK.
METAL OUTBUILDING DESTROYED.
LARGE 30FT APPROX. 2.5 FT DIAMETER CHINABERRY TREE DOWNED ALONG WITH SEVERAL PECAN TREE LIMBS.
MULTIPLE 4-6 INCH TREE LIMBS DOWN.

*** 1 FATAL *** NOLAN COUNTY SHERIFF CONFIRMED 6 ACCIDENTS ON I-20 NEAR ROSCOE, TX AROUND 430PM, WITH 1 FATALITY CAUSED BY A TANKER TRUCK ROLLING OVER. NO TORNADOES OR HAIL REPORTED, BUT LOCALLY STRONG WINDS WERE REPORTED. WINDS AT THE SWEETWATER ASOS WERE 44MPH AND 47MPH AND SWEETWATER 11SW.
*** 1 FATAL *** MULTIPLE CRASHES OCCURRED ON INTERSTATE 20 INCLUDING SEVERAL JACK-KNIFED 18-WHEELERS. HIGHWAY PATROL ALSO REPORTED MULTIPLE DOWNED POWER LINES NEARBY.

WE RECEIVED SEVERAL PICTURES OF FLOODING ON THE EAST SIDE OF SAN ANGELO, THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA, INCLUDING NEAR FORT CONCHO AND NEAR GOODFELLOW AFB. IN ADDITION, WE RECEIVED REPORTS OF STRANDED CARS DUE TO FLOODING, IN THE CITY.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED 3 FEET OF WATER ON THE ROADWAY, WHICH RESULTED IN TWO CARS STALLING ON THE HIGHWAY. ONE OF THE VEHICLES WAS AN 18 WHEELER, WHICH HAD WATER HALF WAY UP THE RADIATOR.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM RULE. A PUBLIC COMMENT FROM FACEBOOK REPORTED WINDS BETWEEN 40 AND 58 MPH IN THE CITY OF RULE.
JIM KORMAN REPORTED WINDS UP TO APPROXIMATELY 58 MPH IN THE CITY OF RULE ON FACEBOOK.
REPORT FROM MPING: 1-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN; SHINGLES BLOWN OFF.
OCCASIONAL QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
RUNNELS COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED COUNTY ROAD 300 (CR300) BETWEEN WINTERS AND FM 1677 IMPASSIBLE DUE TO HIGH WATER.
